content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
August 5,
1996
A. Theodore Giattina
General Counsel
California Housing Finance Agency
1121 L Street,
Seventh Floor
Sacramento, California
95814
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A—96—20l
Dear Mr. Giattina:
This is in response to your request for advice regarding the
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).
1
QUESTION
Under the facts described below, would it be permissible for
the California Housing Finance Agency
(“CHFA”) to distribute
copies of its annual report which include the name, title, written
comments and photograph of the Governor and a photograph of the
State Treasurer along with his name and title?
CONCLUSION
Distribution of over 200 copies of the annual report with the
contents you described would violate the Act’s prohibition on mass
mailings.
You may wish to revise the report to delete the
photograph of the State Treasurer and the photograph, signature,
and comments of the Governor.
FACTS
CHFA is a public instruiuentality and political subdivision of
the State of California which is administered by a board of
directors consisting of
11 voting members.
The Governor appoints
six members of the board and from those appointees selects a
chairperson.
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
Commission regulations
appear at Title 2,
Sections 18000—18995 of the California Code of
Regulations.
zi2
T
OEP’V
cTITTr RflO
r’OAIAPMTfl Cii TIrUMTA QcRla
(Q1f “.‘_6fl


File No.
A—96—201
Page 2
CHFA is self-supporting and does not receive funding for its
operations and programs from the general fund.
Moneys in CHFA’s
housing finance fund are continuously appropriated to
CHFA and
expenditure of the fund is not subject to the supervision or
approval of any other officer or division of state government.
Each year the
CHFA prepares its annual report of programs and
financial operations.
CHFA has prepared an ever—increasing number
of copies of its annual report in recent years and, for the fiscal
year ending June 30,
1996,
intends to prepare approximately 8,000
copies.
The annual report will be distributed as follows:
to persons
and organizations with an interest in affordable housing programs
and finance; in response to unsolicited requests for such
information; as part of information regarding the programs of
CHFA
at the request of persons attending trade shows, conferences and
other expositions attended by CHFA employees; to persons and
institutions in connection with the issuance of CHFA’s debt
obligations and the credit ratings of such obligations;
statutorily mandated distributions of the annual report;
and state
library distribution.
CHFA wishes to include,
as a part of the annual report,
a
photograph of the Governor with the chairman of the CHFA’s board
of directors and the executive director.
A caption to the
photograph would indicate that the chairman of the board and the
executive director are briefing the Governor regarding CHFA’s
operations and performance.
CHFA is considering inclusion of a
short message from the Governor regarding the operations of the
agency, his signature,
and title.
In addition,
CHFA wishes to include in the annual report the
photograph and identification of each of the members of the board
of directors.
One of the statutory members of the board is the
State Treasurer.
ANALYSIS
The Act prohibits the sending of newsletters or other mass
mailings at public expense.
(Section 89001.)
“Mass mailing” means
over two hundred substantially similar pieces of mail, but does
not include mail which is sent in response to an unsolicited
request, letter, or other inquiry, or any items that fall within
the exceptions set forth in Regulation 18901(b).
(Section
82041.5; Regulation 18901(a) (4).)
Regulation 18901(a) provides
that a mailing is prohibited only if all of the following apply:
(1)
Any item sent is delivered,
by any means,
to the recipient at his or her residence, place of
employment or business, or post office box.
For


File No.
A—96—201
Page
3
purposes of this subdivision
(a) (1), the item
delivered to the recipient must be a tangible item,
such as a videotape, record, or button, or a
written document.
(2)
The item sent either:
(A)
Features an elected officer
affiliated with the agency which produces or
sends the mailing,
or
(B)
Includes the name, office,
photograph, or other reference to an elected
officer affiliated with the agency which
produces or sends the mailing,
and is prepared
or sent in cooperation, consultation,
coordination, or concert with the elected
officer.
(3) (A)
Any of the costs of distribution is
paid for with public moneys; or
(B)
Costs of design, production,
and
printing exceeding $50.00 are paid with public
moneys,
and the design, production,
or
printing is done with the intent of sending
the item other than as permitted by this
regulation.
(4)
More than two hundred substantially
similar items are sent,
in a single calendar month,
excluding any item sent in response to an
unsolicited request and any item described in
subdivision (b).
Regulation 18901(a).
The mass mailing prohibition applies to the distribution of
CHFA’s annual report as you have described it.
Costs of
distributing the annual report or costs exceeding $50 for
designing, producing, or printing the report will be paid for with
public moneys.
2
CHFA will be sending over 200 copies of the
report to persons and organizations interested in affordable
2
By reference to Penal Code Section 426,
Section 85102(e)
defines “public moneys” to include all bonds and evidence of
indebtedness,
and all moneys belonging to the state,
or any city,
county,
town, district,
or public agency therein,
and all moneys,
bonds,
and evidence of indebtedness received or held by state,
county, district, city, town,
or public agency officers in their
official capacity.


File No.
A—96—201
Page 4
housing programs and finance.
And the annual report features
elected officers affiliated with CHFA.
An elected officer is “affiliated” with an agency if the
officer is a member, officer, or employee of the agency or a
subunit
(such as a committee)
of that agency; has supervisory
control over the agency; or appoints any of the members of the
agency.
(Regulation 18901(c) (1); Riddle Advice Letter,
No.
A-89—096.)
The State Treasurer is affiliated with CHFA because he
is a member of the board of directors.
The Governor is affiliated
with CHFA because he appoints six members of the board.
If the mailing has not been prepared or sent in cooperation,
consultation, coordination, or concert with the elected officer,
use of the elected officer’s name is permitted provided the
officer is not featured in the mailing.
(Regulation
18901 (a) (2) (A).)
An elected officer is “featured” in a mass
mailing if he or she is singled out for the attention of the
reader by use of his or her signature,
inclusion in any
photograph,
or the manner of display of his or her name or office
in the layout of the document such as by headlines, type size,
or
typeface.
(Regulation 18901(c) (2).)
The inclusion in CHFA’s
annual report of the photograph of the State Treasurer and the
photograph and signed comments of the Governor would be
“featuring” these elected officers.
Therefore,
sending more than
200 of the reports in a calendar month would constitute a
prohibited mass mailing under the Act.
(See Hodson Advice Letter,
No.
1—94—301; Moore Advice Letter,
No. A-93-307; and Miller Advice
Letter,
No.
1—93—266.)
Parts of the proposed distribution of the annual report come
within exceptions to the mass mailing prohibition.
For example,
reports sent in response to unsolicited requests, reports picked
up by individuals attending trade shows
3
, reports sent per
statutory mandate,
and reports sent to state libraries, would not
be covered by the mass mailing prohibition.
(Regulation
18901 (a) (4),
(b) (3),
and
(b) (7).)
Because CHFA plans to distribute 8,000 copies of the annual
report, however, it appears that the agency would send more than
200 reports in a calendar month to individuals or organizations
that do not fit within execeptions to the mass mailing
prohibition.
To proceed with a widespread distribution of the
annual report,
CHFA would need to delete the photograph of the
State Treasurer and the photograph, signature,
and comments of the
Governor.
CHFA’s revised annual report may include the name of the
State Treasurer as a boardmember,
so long as he is not featured.
If items are set out for the public to pick up on their own,
or are handed out in a public area, the restrictions of the
regulation do not apply.


File No.
A—96--201
Page 5
For example, the State Treasurer’s name must appear in the same
typeface and size as the other boardinembers’ names.
(See Steffen
Advice Letter,
No. A-90-033.)
In addition, the annual report may
include a reference to the Governor so long as he is not featured,
and the report is not prepared or sent with his cooperation.
(Regulation 18901(a) (2).)
I trust this answers your question.
If you have any further
questions regarding this matter, please contact me at 916/322-
5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counsel
-h2tL
P,
By:
Hyla P.
Wagner
Counsel,
Legal Division
SGC:HPW:ak

and comments of the Governor.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Under the facts described below, would it be permissible for
the California Housing Finance Agency
(“CHFA”) to distribute
copies of its annual report which include the name, title, written
comments and photograph of the Governor and a photograph of the
State Treasurer along with his name and title?

CONCLUSION: Distribution of over 200 copies of the annual report with the
contents you described would violate the Act’s prohibition on mass
mailings.
You may wish to revise the report to delete the
photograph of the State Treasurer and the photograph, signature,
and comments of the Governor.
